step2_verification.qa_pairs:
1. [SUPPORTED] Q: Does the document associate the domain "mail-updateservice[.]info" with threat-actor activity (e.g. phishing, C2, or impersonation)?
   A: Yes, the document associates the domain "mail-updateservice[.]info" with threat-actor activity, specifically as a C2 (command-and-control) server used by the BellaCiao malware.
2. [SUPPORTED] Q: Does the document associate the domain "msn-center[.]uk" with threat-actor activity (e.g. phishing, C2, or impersonation)?
   A: Yes, the document associates the domain "msn-center[.]uk" with threat-actor activity by listing it as a source in the table of indicators of compromise under the category "Network", indicating that Bitdefender research has identified this domain as being used for malicious purposes.
3. [SUPPORTED] Q: Does the document associate the domain "msn-service[.]co" with threat-actor activity (e.g. phishing, C2, or impersonation)?
   A: Yes, the document associates the domain "msn-service[.]co" with threat-actor activity by listing it as a source in the table of indicators of compromise under the Network section.
4. [SUPPORTED] Q: Does the document associate the domain "twittsupport[.]com" with threat-actor activity (e.g. phishing, C2, or impersonation)?
   A: Yes, the document associates the domain "twittsupport[.]com" with threat-actor activity by listing it as a source in the "Network" section under "Domain".
5. [SUPPORTED] Q: Does the document associate the domain "mailupdate[.]info" with threat-actor activity (e.g. phishing, C2, or impersonation)?
   A: Yes, the document associates the domain "mailupdate[.]info" with threat-actor activity as a C2 server.
6. [SUPPORTED] Q: Does the document associate the domain "maill-support[.]com" with threat-actor activity (e.g. phishing, C2, or impersonation)?
   A: Yes, the document associates the domain "maill-support[.]com" with threat-actor activity by listing it as a C2 server in the table of indicators of compromise.
7. [SUPPORTED] Q: Does the document attribute the file hash "5a487c41efa2f3055d641591d601977c" to a specific malware sample, dropper, or malicious tool?
   A: Yes, the document attributes the file hash "5a487c41efa2f3055d641591d601977c" to a build of IIS-Raid, a native IIS module.
8. [SUPPORTED] Q: Does the document attribute the file hash "95c6fdc4f537bccca3079d94e65bc0b0" to a specific malware sample, dropper, or malicious tool?
   A: Yes, the document attributes the file hash "95c6fdc4f537bccca3079d94e65bc0b0" to a .NET IIS module for credential exfiltration.
9. [SUPPORTED] Q: Does the document attribute the file hash "3fbea74b92f41809f46145f480782ef9" to a specific malware sample, dropper, or malicious tool?
   A: Yes, the document attributes the file hash "3fbea74b92f41809f46145f480782ef9" to the Plink tool used for establishing reverse proxy connections to the C2 server.
10. [SUPPORTED] Q: Does the document describe "BellaCiao" as a tool, malware, or utility used BY the threat actor(s) in this report (not a defensive or victim tool)?
   A: Yes, the document describes "BellaCiao" as a malware used by the threat actor(s), specifically an Iranian state-sponsored APT group associated with the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC).
11. [SUPPORTED] Q: Does the document describe "IIS-Raid" as a tool, malware, or utility used BY the threat actor(s) in this report (not a defensive or victim tool)?
   A: Yes, the document describes "IIS-Raid" as a native IIS module used by the threat actor(s) to process every IIS request and look for pre-defined headers with password and command to execute.
12. [SUPPORTED] Q: Does the document describe "Plink" as a tool, malware, or utility used BY the threat actor(s) in this report (not a defensive or victim tool)?
   A: Yes, the document describes "Plink" as a tool used by the threat actor(s) to establish reverse proxy connections to the C2 server.
